perturbation.original_text: All fees or expenses due to the Agent but unpaid will be payable to the Agent in next day funds at the earlier of the Closing Date (as hereinafter defined) or the End Date. In the event the Offering is extended beyond the End Date, the Company, the MHC, the Bank and the Agent may agree to renew this Agreement under mutually acceptable terms.
perturbation.changed_text: All fees or expenses due to the Agent but unpaid will be payable to the Agent in next day funds at the earlier of the Closing Date (as hereinafter defined) or 30 days after the End Date. In the event the Offering is extended beyond the End Date, the Company, the MHC, the Bank and the Agent may agree to renew this Agreement under mutually acceptable terms.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the payment deadline for fees and expenses. Originally, unpaid fees were due at the Closing Date or the End Date (45 days after Closing Date), whichever is sooner. The modification changes this to be due either on the Closing Date, or 30 days *after* the End Date. This directly contradicts the original intention and introduces uncertainty regarding payment terms.This is contradictory to the closing, since the closing date is mentioned at Section 14, implying it will take place at the original closing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The closing for the sale of the Shares shall take place on the Closing Date at such location as mutually agreed upon by the Agent and the Company, the MHC and the Bank. At the closing, the Company, the MHC and the Bank shall deliver to the Agent in next day funds the commissions, fees and expenses due and owing to the Agent as set forth in Sections 2 and 6 hereof and the opinions and certificates required hereby and other documents deemed reasonably necessary by the Agent shall be executed and delivered to effect the sale of the Shares as contemplated hereby and pursuant to the terms of the Prospectus.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that fees are payable at the earlier of the Closing Date or the End Date. The changed text alters this, making fees payable at the earlier of the Closing Date or 30 days *after* the End Date. The Contradicted Text states that fees will be paid on the Closing Date. This change introduces a direct contradiction as it creates a scenario where fees that were due on the Closing Date per the Contradicted Text might not be due until 30 days *after* the End Date due to the Changed Text, causing an Inconsistency - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Whether or not the Offering is completed or the sale of the Shares by the Company is consummated, the Company, the MHC and the Bank jointly and severally agree to pay or reimburse the Agent for the Company, the MHC and the Bank have agreed to reimburse the Agent for its out-of-pocket expenses, and its legal fees (as specified in Section 2) and to indemnify the Agent against certain claims or liabilities, including certain liabilities under the Securities Act, and will contribute to payments the Agent may be required to make in connection with any such claims or liabilities; and the fees set forth under Section 2. In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 708,050 Shares, the Company, the MHC and the Bank shall promptly reimburse the Agent in accordance with Section 2 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Whether or not the Offering is completed or the sale of the Shares by the Company is consummated, the Company, the MHC and the Bank jointly and severally agree to pay or reimburse the Agent for its out-of-pocket expenses, and its legal fees (as specified in Section 2) and to indemnify the Agent against certain claims or liabilities, including certain liabilities under the Securities Act, and will contribute to payments the Agent may be required to make in connection with any such claims or liabilities; and the fees set forth under Section 2. In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 1,000,000 Shares, the Company, the MHC and the Bank shall promptly reimburse the Agent in accordance with Section 2 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the minimum number of shares that must be sold for the Company to reimburse the Agent, from 708,050 shares to 1,000,000. This creates an in-text contradiction since it affects whether or not reimbursement is due to occur. Also Section 2 states "In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 708,050 Shares within the period herein provided, this Agreement shall terminate and the Company shall refund to any persons who have subscribed for any of the Shares, the full amount which it may have received from them plus accrued interest as set forth in the Prospectus; and none of the parties to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other parties hereunder, except as set forth in this Section 2 and in Sections 6, 8 and 9 hereof." This makes Section 2 contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 708,050 Shares within the period herein provided, this Agreement shall terminate and the Company shall refund to any persons who have subscribed for any of the Shares, the full amount which it may have received from them plus accrued interest as set forth in the Prospectus; and none of the parties to this Agreement shall have any obligation to the other parties hereunder, except as set forth in this Section 2 and in Sections 6, 8 and 9 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the minimum number of shares required to be sold from 708,050 to 1,000,000. The contradicted text states that if the Company is unable to sell a minimum of 708,050 Shares, the agreement will terminate. The changed text creates an inconsistency, because now the trigger for termination is a different number of shares, making the original clause in the contradicted text unenforceable as originally written.
